# 'downstack merge' fetches trunk after merge,
# ensuring upstack branches are retargeted onto the updated trunk
# and retain the merged branch's changes for an explicit restack.

as 'Test <test@example.com>'
at '2024-04-05T16:40:32Z'

# setup
cd repo
git init
git config spice.experiment.merge true
git commit --allow-empty -m 'Initial commit'

# set up a fake GitHub remote
shamhub init
shamhub new origin alice/example.git
shamhub register alice
git push origin main

env SHAMHUB_USERNAME=alice
gs auth login

# create a stack: main -> feature1 -> feature2
# feature2 modifies the same file as feature1
# to demonstrate that the merge doesn't lose feature1's changes.
git add feature1.txt
gs bc feature1 -m 'Add feature 1'
git add feature2.txt
gs bc feature2 -m 'Add feature 2'

# submit the stack
gs downstack submit --fill
stderr 'Created #1'
stderr 'Created #2'

# merge only feature1 from feature1
gs bco feature1
env ROBOT_INPUT=$WORK/robot-merge.golden ROBOT_OUTPUT=$WORK/robot-merge.actual
gs downstack merge
cmp $WORK/robot-merge.actual $WORK/robot-merge.golden
stderr 'feature1: merging #1:'
stderr 'feature2: retargeted upstack onto main'
stderr 'feature1: #1 was merged'
stderr 'feature1: deleted'

# Switch to feature2 and verify it contains feature1's changes.
# If trunk was not fetched before the upstack rebase,
# feature1.txt would be missing from feature2.
gs bco feature2
exec cat feature1.txt
stdout 'This is feature 1'
exec cat feature2.txt
stdout 'This is feature 2'

# The upstack branch is left for an explicit restack.
gs branch restack
stderr 'feature2: restacked on main'

-- repo/feature1.txt --
This is feature 1
-- repo/feature2.txt --
This is feature 2
-- robot-merge.golden --
===
> Merge 1 change(s) bottom-up?: [Y/n]
>   feature1 (#1)
true
